• **Mentalidad de Rebaño**: Cuando gigantes tecnológicos como Broadcom alcanzan máximos históricos, los inversores minoristas se apresuran a comprar, a menudo en precios máximos
• **Herdenmentalität**: Wenn Tech-Giganten wie Broadcom Allzeithochs erreichen, stürzen sich Kleinanleger auf Käufe, oft zu Höchstpreisen
• **Herd Mentality**: When tech giants like Broadcom hit all-time highs, retail investors rush to buy, often at peak prices

At current prices, a four pack of H100 94GB's is going to cost you well north of $180,000. That's without the system to install them in, which can run anywhere from $35k to north of $75k.

That's what people often assume, but GPUs are a lot more expensive than people realize. 4x H100 94GB by themselves cost SUBSTANTIALLY more than a top-end system optimized for FEA excluding any FPGA or accelerators and assuming 2s, 384c, 1-1.5TB DDR5.

And ANY serious physics work requires double-precision minimum. Non-sparse FP32 and FP64 are not optional. The H100 going FP16 to FP64 is a minimum 90% drop in performance.
Blackwell? MAXIMUM of 0.7% FP64 performance compared to H100. NOT a typo or math error.

Most of my systems are used in very, VERY demanding physics work. And what keeps being found is that every pro-GPU benchmark (especially H100) lied, AND when confirmation was attempted? VERY significant errors were found. And it couldn't perform real workloads at ALL.

For a model like Llama we need to train on around 15 trillion tokens. 1 H100 can work on 1.9B tokens in about 20.5 days. Therefore to train the model in 20.5 days we need to rent 7680 H100 GPUs running in parallel. Total cost: 7680 * 20.5d * 24h * $4 = about $15M

For large deployments of hundreds of GPUs we have higher costs per hour than a single deployment (<$2) of one H100 GPU, due to additional equipment, networking etc. Roughly around 4$ per Hour and GPU, meaning it costs us $288 per hour (72 x $4) in our example.

For our reference calculation, let's use a pretty capable open-source model (Llama 3.1 70b) running on 72 H100 Nvidia GPUs and token input and output ratio of about 60% and 40% (input tokens usually keep growing during an ongoing conversation, hence ~60%).
